page.content: In Deutsche Bank's Fiscal Year 2024 report, the Credit Card Delinquency Trends Overview reveals delinquency rates and amounts outstanding segmented by age group.

lines.text: In Deutsche Bank's Fiscal Year 2024 report, the Credit Card Delinquency Trends Overview reveals delinquency rates and
amounts outstanding segmented by age group.

page.content: For Q1 2024, the delinquency rates were 1.5% for the 18-24 years age group with an amount outstanding of $1.2B, 2.3% for 25-34 years at $1.5B, 3.0% for 35-44 years at $0.8B, and 2.1% for 45+ years at $0.6B.

lines.text: For Q1 2024, the delinquency rates were 1.5% for the 18-24 years age group with an amount outstanding of $1.2B, 2.3% for
25-34 years at $1.5B, 3.0% for 35-44 years at $0.8B, and 2.1% for 45+ years at $0.6B.

page.content: In Q2 2024, rates increased to 1.7% ($1.3B) for 18-24 years, 2.5% ($1.6B) for 25-34 years, 3.2% ($0.9B) for 35-44 years, and 2.3% ($0.7B) for 45+ years. Q3 2024 showed further deterioration with rates at 1.9% ($1.4B) for 18-24 years, 2.7% ($1.8B) for 25-34 years, 3.5% ($1.0B) for 35-44 years, and 2.5% ($0.8B) for 45+ years. Q4 2024 concluded with rates of 2.9% ($1.9B) for 18-24 years, 3.8% ($1.1B) for 25-34 years, and 2.6% ($0.9B) for 35-44 years. The total average rate was 2.0% with an aggregate amount of $1.5B. The Capital Adequacy Metrics for 2024 show that the Tier 1 Capital is $16,000M with a YoY change of 5.0%, Common Equity Tier 1 at $14,000M (4.5%), Risk-Weighted Assets at $22,000M (2.0%), and Market Share at 5.0%. The Regulatory Requirements target a minimum ratio of 12.5%, with an actual ratio of 15.0%, indicating excess capital of $7,000M and total capital ratio of 18.0% with a leverage ratio of 4.0%.
